transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Patricia.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi, I'm Alexis. I've been sober for six months and I've started drinking again. I went to rehab two years ago and it obviously didn't work.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for telling me that, Alexis. Six months of sobriety is real — that's not nothing. Relapse doesn't erase that. Can I ask: when you say rehab 'didn't work,' what does that mean to you? What were you hoping would happen that didn't?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I thought I'd be fixed. I went in, did the 30 days, came out, and eventually ended up back where I started.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's a really honest answer. The thing I want to offer you — not as reassurance, but as a different frame — is that 30-day residential works for some people and doesn't for others, and that's about fit, not failure. There's research showing that people who relapse after treatment and re-enter treatment have better long-term outcomes than those who never return. You coming back is actually part of the process, not outside of it.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Most people don't. So the question isn't whether treatment can work for you — it's whether this time we design it differently. What did the last program not give you that you think you needed?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Aftercare. I left and there was nothing. No follow-up, no support group, nothing.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's one of the most common reasons people relapse — and it's something we specifically address. Our aftercare planning starts on day one, not day thirty. I want to make a note of that for our clinical team right now. Alexis, do you have insurance?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. Summit Select. Member ID BP63244887, group GRP-514222.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. I'm going to verify your benefits and flag the aftercare concern for your intake coordinator. They'll walk you through how our continuing care program works. Can they reach you this afternoon?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You'll hear from us before three. Alexis — coming back after a relapse is one of the hardest things to do. The fact that you made this call tells me something important about you.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thank you. I hope it's different this time.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: We'll make sure the plan is.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Strong objection handling — agent reframed relapse as part of process with genuine evidence, then asked a powerful open question about what was missing (not 'what went wrong'). Correctly identified aftercare as the gap and promised to flag it for intake. The research framing was accurate and non-patronising. Clinical screen was lighter than ideal — only the relapse/drinking history was covered.
